engineCodes.h2: Range Rover Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Range Rover engine — Ingenium diesel, TDV6/SDV6, and V8 petrol. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Range Rover specialists.

groups.name: Ingenium Four-Cylinder Diesel
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: Ingenium 2.0 diesel engines are known for timing chain stretch/failure and oil dilution caused by incomplete DPF regenerations . Our rebuilt units include upgraded timing chain kits, revised oil pumps, and thoroughly cleaned DPF systems.

groups.name: TDV6 / SDV6 V6 Diesel
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: TDV6/SDV6 engines are notorious for catastrophic crankshaft failure due to narrow bearing design . Our rebuilt units include uprated bearings, revised oil pumps, and tested turbo actuator assemblies.

groups.name: V6 Petrol
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: The 3.0 V6 supercharged petrol engine suffers from timing chain tensioner wear and supercharger coupler degradation. Our rebuilt units include reinforced tensioners and revised supercharger components.

groups.name: V8 Petrol
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: 5.0 V8 supercharged engines suffer from timing chain tensioner failure and water pump leaks. Our rebuilt units include reinforced tensioners, revised oil pumps, and upgraded supercharger couplers.

groups.name: TDV8 / SDV8 V8 Diesel
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: TDV8 and SDV8 engines are known for crankshaft fracture, timing chain tensioner wear, and turbo actuator faults. Our rebuilt units address all known weaknesses with upgraded bearings and revised turbo components.

groups.name: Hybrid / PHEV
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: P400e hybrid systems combine 2.0 Ingenium petrol engine with electric motor. Failures include timing chain issues and hybrid system faults. Specialist hybrid diagnosis required — our network includes qualified hybrid technicians.

groups.name: Classic / BMW-Sourced Engines
groups.failureNote: Get Quote: Common failure points: P38-era Rover V8 engines suffer from cylinder liner slippage and cooling system issues. Our rebuilt units include liner fixes and upgraded gaskets

commonProblems.h2: Common Range Rover Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Range Rover ownership brings an unmatched blend of luxury and capability, but certain engine families have developed well-documented, high-stakes failure patterns. The 3.0 SDV6/TDV6 diesel’s crankshaft failures and the 5.0 V8 supercharged petrol’s timing chain issues dominate replacement searches, while Ingenium 2.0 units and 4.4 TDV8 diesels present their own costly failures. Every reconditioned engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common failures, what they cost to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement becomes the only sensible option.

problems.h4: 3.0 SDV6 / TDV6 Crankshaft Failure – The Catastrophic Weakness
problems.rootCause: Narrow main bearing journals and inadequate oil flow to the rear crankshaft web cause fatigue cracking. Crankshaft snaps without warning, often sending conrods through the block — instant engine death.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Complete replacement engine with uprated crankshaft bearings, revised oil pump, new timing chains, and full bench testing. Supply & fit by specialist. | 80,000 – 120,000+ miles when properly maintained. The only realistic option after a snapped crank.

| Used take-out engine from a donor vehicle. Typically 50-80k miles. No upgraded components. Fitted by specialist. | 40,000 – 80,000 miles maximum. Risk of repeat failure. Budget option best for short-term ownership.

problems.recommendation: Once the crankshaft has snapped, there is no minor repair. The engine is scrap. For vehicles you plan to keep beyond two years, invest in a reconditioned SDV6 engine with uprated bearings. It costs more upfront but eliminates the factory flaw. Used engines carry the same design risk and often fail again within 40,000 miles. On a Range Rover worth £12,000–£25,000, a properly reconditioned engine gives you another 100,000 miles of life.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned 3.0 SDV6 engine prices for your L405 or L494 now

problems.h4: 5.0 V8 Supercharged Timing Chain Failure – The High‑Mileage Threat
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover (L322 2010-2012, L405 2013-2017), Range Rover Sport (L494 2014-2017, L320 2010-2013) — 5.0 V8 Supercharged (508PN)
problems.rootCause: Overly long 15,000-mile oil change intervals degrade the timing chain tensioners. Chains stretch, guides crack, and the engine jumps timing — bending valves and destroying pistons.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Full timing chain kit with reinforced tensioners, guides, sprockets, and new oil pump. Engine stays in the car. Labour-intensive. | 80,000 – 100,000 miles. Only viable if caught early before valve damage occurs. Perfect preventative fix.

| Complete reconditioned engine with upgraded timing chain components, reinforced tensioners, new supercharger coupler, and bench testing. Supply & fit. | 100,000+ miles. Required if timing has already jumped or chains have failed completely.

problems.recommendation: If your 5.0 V8 starts rattling on cold start-up and you catch it before the check engine light flashes, the timing chain replacement is the smarter financial move. Specialist pricing around £2,200-£3,200 is a fraction of replacement cost. However, if the engine has already jumped timing or metal debris is in the oil, go straight to a reconditioned unit with upgraded tensioners. Don’t throw good money after bad.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 5.0 V8 supercharged engine prices with upgraded timing components

problems.h4: 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 Crankshaft & Turbo Actuator Failures – The Long‑Distance Burden
problems.rootCause: Crankshaft fractures at the rear journal due to harmonic balancer fatigue. Simultaneously, secondary turbo actuator gears strip, causing ‘restricted performance’ mode. Coolant crossover pipes leak, leading to sudden overheating.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Turbo Actuator & Coolant Pipe Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace secondary turbo actuator assembly, fit revised coolant crossover pipes, and full system flush. No engine removal. | 30,000 – 50,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if only actuator/pipe issues present and no crank symptoms.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Reconditioned engine with revised crankshaft, new turbo actuators, upgraded coolant pipes, and full bench testing. Supply & fit. | 100,000+ miles. Only realistic fix for crankshaft failure.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a knocking noise from the bottom end or the engine feels unbalanced, suspect crankshaft fracture immediately. Stop driving and fit a reconditioned unit. If your only symptoms are reduced power and a coolant leak, the actuator and pipe replacement is a cost-effective fix. But if the vehicle has covered over 110,000 miles and you plan to tow or cover long distances, pre-emptively replacing the engine with a reconditioned SDV8 unit gives you peace of mind for years.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for reconditioned 4.4 TDV8 engines with revised crankshafts

problems.h4: 2.0 Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Stretch – The Out‑of‑Warranty Shock
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Evoque (2015-2019), Range Rover Velar (2017-2020) — 2.0 Ingenium Diesel (204DTD, AJ200D)
problems.rootCause: Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regenerations degrades the timing chain and guides. Chains stretch, jump teeth, and the engine loses compression. Piston ring design contributes to oil contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Full timing chain kit with upgraded chains, guides, tensioners, and revised oil pump. Engine remains fitted. | 60,000 – 80,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before chain jumps or tensioner fails completely.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Timing chain replacement plus cylinder head rebuild, new piston rings, DPF clean, and oil system flush. Engine removed. | 80,000 – 100,000+ miles. Required if chain has jumped and valves have contacted pistons.
| Complete reconditioned engine with upgraded timing chain kit, revised piston rings, new oil pump, and bench testing. Supply & fit. | 80,000 – 120,000 miles. Best value when damage is extensive.

problems.recommendation: On a 2.0 Ingenium, timing chain rattle on cold start needs immediate attention. A preventative chain and guide replacement at a specialist for £1,500-£2,200 is significantly cheaper than a full rebuild. However, if the engine has already lost power and the check engine light is flashing for timing correlation faults, skip the rebuild and go straight to a reconditioned unit. On an Evoque or Velar worth £8,000-£15,000, a replacement engine with upgraded components gives you a like-new drivetrain for half the dealer’s replacement cost.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned 2.0 Ingenium engine prices for your Evoque or Velar

problems.h4: P400e Hybrid System & Engine Failures – The Emerging Problem
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover (L405 2019-2021, L460 2022+), Range Rover Sport (L494 2019-2022) — P400e 2.0 Plug-in Hybrid (PT204 petrol + electric motor)
problems.rootCause: Electric motor bearings fail, contaminating the engine oil with metal debris. The 2.0 Ingenium petrol engine suffers high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure, timing chain stretch, and coolant loss from the hybrid battery thermal management system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace high-pressure fuel pump, fit upgraded timing chain kit, oil and filter change. Engine remains in car. | 40,000 – 60,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if electric motor is not contaminated.

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned P400e Engine + Motor
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Reconditioned 2.0 petrol engine with upgraded chain and HPFP, plus reconditioned electric motor unit. Specialist hybrid system testing. Supply & fit. | 80,000+ miles. Required if motor bearings have failed or oil is contaminated with metal.

problems.recommendation: P400e hybrid failures are the new frontier. If your Range Rover shows a hybrid system fault, unusual whining from the electric motor, or blackened engine oil, stop driving immediately. Do not attempt a minor HPFP or chain repair without having the motor and oil system inspected for metal debris. Once contaminated, the only safe route is a full replacement engine and motor package. Because fewer specialists handle hybrids, using EngineMarket to compare P400e-ready specialists will save you thousands over main dealer pricing.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for reconditioned P400e hybrid engine and motor packages

problems.h4: 2.7 TDV6 Oil Pump Housing Failure – The Silent Killer
problems.rootCause: Plastic oil pump housing fractures, causing sudden oil pressure loss. The engine seizes within seconds. No warning lights precede failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace plastic oil pump housing with revised aluminium unit, new timing belt, and full oil flush. Engine in situ. | 80,000+ miles. Only viable if done before housing fractures. Proactive owners only.

| Reconditioned engine with upgraded aluminium oil pump housing, new timing belts, bearings, and bench testing. Supply & fit. | 80,000 – 120,000 miles. Only realistic option after sudden oil loss and seizure.

problems.recommendation: The 2.7 TDV6’s plastic oil pump housing is a ticking clock. If your Sport L320 is over 70,000 miles and the housing is original, spend £1,000 at a specialist to fit the upgraded aluminium version. This preventative fix saves the engine. However, if you’ve already experienced sudden oil pressure loss — usually accompanied by a bang and complete engine stop — the engine is seized. Only a reconditioned unit with the aluminium housing will give you reliable service.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned 2.7 TDV6 engine prices with upgraded oil pump housing

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Range Rover
finalCta.paragraph: Every engine family in the Range Rover range has a known failure point — but none of them need to end your ownership. Whether you own a crankshaft-failure-prone SDV6, a timing-chain-weary 5.0 V8, or an Ingenium diesel with stretched chains, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every reconditioned engine through our network includes upgraded components that address the factory flaw, a full bench test, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today and get your Range Rover back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Range Rover Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Range Rover model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Range Rover Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Range Rover engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle’s age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Range Rover Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £2,500 – £6,500 (depending on engine code)
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Range Rover engine prices

types.title: Range Rover Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated SDV6 crankshaft bearings and reinforced 5.0 V8 timing chain tensioners – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.
types.cta: Get rebuilt Range Rover engine quotes

types.title: Used Range Rover Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.
types.cta: Check used Range Rover engine availability

types.title: Range Rover Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Range Rover engine costs

types.title: Range Rover Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn’t had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a full recon isn’t economical.
types.cta: View refurbished Range Rover engine options

types.title: Range Rover Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Range Rover specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £500 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Range Rover engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Range Rover model today.

engineSizes.h2: Range Rover Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Range Rover engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Range Rover 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel entered the Range Rover family with the Evoque and Velar as part of JLR’s Ingenium family. These engines replaced older Ford-derived units and prioritised efficiency, but timing chain stretch and oil dilution from DPF regenerations became widespread failure points. While fuel economy impresses, catastrophic chain failure often writes off vehicles out of warranty
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2-litre diesel arrived via Ford’s Duratorq family, fitted almost exclusively to the first-generation Evoque. While more robust than early Ingenium units, these engines suffer from EGR valve clogging and turbocharger oil feed restrictions. Most replacement demand comes from high-mileage examples where the turbo has failed and sent debris through the engine
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre diesel belongs to the P38 generation, powered by BMW’s M51 straight-six. This is a classic engine – mechanically robust but now aged. Replacement demand comes from head gasket failure and injection pump seal leaks on high-mileage or poorly maintained examples. Few specialists still rebuild these, but used units remain available
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 2.5L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: cracked cylinder heads on overheated units

items.title: Range Rover 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre diesel was the first TDV6, fitted to the early Sport L320 and the L322 full-size Range Rover. Ford and PSA co-developed this engine, but the plastic oil pump housing is its Achilles’ heel – when it fractures, oil pressure vanishes and the engine seizes without warning. Most replacement demand comes from sudden, catastrophic oil loss
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre diesel is the most common and most failure-prone engine in modern Range Rovers. The TDV6, SDV6, and later Ingenium six-cylinder diesels all share a devastating weakness: crankshaft bearing failure leading to a snapped crank. This is the single largest replacement driver across L322, L405, L494, and L460 models. Reconditioned units with uprated bearings are the only long-term fix
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 3.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.6-litre TDV8 was the first V8 diesel in a Range Rover, fitted exclusively to the L322. This Ford-Lion unit is smoother than the TDV6 but has its own demons: crankshaft bearing wear, turbo actuator failure, and EGR cooler leaks. Replacement demand comes mostly from high-mileage towing vehicles where the bottom end has fatigued
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 3.6L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 4.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 4.4-litre TDV8 and later SDV8 represent the pinnacle of Range Rover diesel engineering – but they are not immune to failure. Crankshaft fracture at the rear journal, secondary turbo actuator gear stripping, and coolant crossover pipe leaks are well-documented. Replacement is expensive, but reconditioned units with revised crankshafts and upgraded actuators offer a permanent fix
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover 4.4L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol arrived with the Ingenium family in the Evoque and Velar, later appearing as the combustion half of the P400e plug-in hybrid. These engines are prone to high-pressure fuel pump failure and timing chain stretch. Replacement demand is rising as these vehicles age out of warranty and main dealer quotes shock owners
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 2.0L petrol engine prices
compatibleModels: Range Rover Sport L494 (2019–2022 – P400e hybrid)

items.title: Range Rover 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre petrol – both supercharged V6 and later mild-hybrid inline-six – appears in higher-spec L405 and L494 models. The supercharged V6 suffers timing chain tensioner wear, while the later Ingenium petrol six is still proving its reliability. Replacement demand currently centres on earlier supercharged units where chain rattle has been ignored until catastrophic failure
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0-litre Rover V8 belongs to the P38 generation – a pushrod, naturally aspirated engine that is simple by modern standards. Replacement demand comes from age-related failures: slipped cylinder liners, camshaft wear, and cracked distributor caps. Rebuilt units with top-hat liners solve the liner slip issue permanently
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2-litre supercharged V8 appeared in the Sport L320 and late L322 as a performance-oriented option. Based on the 4.4-litre naturally aspirated V8, the added supercharger stresses the timing chain guides and tensioners. Replacement demand comes from chain failure and supercharger coupler wear
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.4-litre naturally aspirated V8 – codenamed AJ41 – powered early L322 models and is widely considered one of the more reliable Range Rover petrol engines. Still, age takes its toll: VANOS units fail, timing chain guides become brittle, and valley pan gaskets leak. Replacement is uncommon, but when it happens, reconditioned units are the standard
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 4.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 4.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.6-litre Rover V8 was the larger displacement option in the P38 generation, offering more torque for towing. It shares the 4.0-litre’s Achilles’ heel: cylinder liner slippage when overheated. Replacement demand comes from owners who want to preserve a classic – rebuilt units with top-hat liners are the gold standard
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 4.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.0-litre V8 – both naturally aspirated and supercharged – is the flagship Range Rover petrol engine. It delivers effortless power but suffers from timing chain tensioner wear and, on supercharged variants, coupler failure. Replacement demand is high among owners of L322, L405, and L494 models where chain rattle has been ignored until valve timing jumps. Reconditioned units with reinforced tensioners offer permanent peace of mind
items.cta: Compare Range Rover 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover 2.0L Hybrid Engines (P400e Plug-in Hybrid)
items.description: The P400e combines a 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor, fitted to L405, L494, and L460 models. Electric motor bearing failure can contaminate the engine oil with metal debris, while the hybrid battery thermal management system is prone to coolant leaks. Specialist diagnosis is essential – few general garages understand this system. System / motor code(s): PT204 petrol engine + unnamed 105kW rear-axle motor
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Range Rover 3.0L MHEV (Mild Hybrid)
items.description: The 3.0-litre Ingenium petrol six-cylinder mild hybrid (MHEV) adds a belt-integrated starter generator (BiSG) to reduce emissions. While generally more reliable than plug-in hybrids, the BiSG unit can fail, and the complex 48V electrical system requires specialist diagnostic equipment. Replacement of the engine or BiSG component is rare but expensive when required. System / motor code(s): AJ20P6 (3.0 petrol) + BiSG unit
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover 3.0L mild hybrid availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Range Rover needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Range Rover specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Range Rover Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Range Rover engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.

items.title: Range Rover Diesel Engines
items.description: Range Rover diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
knownFor: Timing chain stretch
oil dilution
DPF regeneration issues
high-pressure fuel pump failure
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover diesel engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Petrol Engines
items.description: Range Rover petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
knownFor: High-pressure fuel pump failure
timing chain stretch
piston ring carbon buildup
Timing chain tensioner wear (supercharged V6)
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Hybrid Engines
items.description: Range Rover hybrid powertrains combine combustion engines with electric assistance across newer electrified models. Replacement demand is lower than traditional petrol or diesel units, but generator-engine faults, inverter issues, cooling-system failures, and calibration-sensitive repairs still require specialist support.
importantNotes: Hybrid faults often need both mechanical and high-voltage diagnosis, so replacement decisions should be made by specialists familiar with the full system.
Battery cooling, inverter health, and software calibration are just as important as the combustion-engine condition on hybrid models.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover hybrid engine prices

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Range Rover has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Range Rover specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Range Rover Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Range Rover engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Range Rover specialists.

entries.title: 306DT 3.0 TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The 306DT powered the Range Rover Sport (L320, 2009–2013) and full-size Range Rover (L322, 2009–2012). Catastrophic crankshaft failure due to narrow bearing design is the dominant replacement driver, typically between 80,000 and 130,000 miles. A quality rebuilt unit includes uprated crankshaft bearings, a revised oil pump, and tested turbo actuators.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DT Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 306DTX 3.0 SDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The twin‑turbo 306DTX is found in the Range Rover (L405, 2013–2017), Range Rover Sport (L494, 2013–2017), and Discovery 4/5. The same crankshaft bearing fatigue affects this higher‑output variant, often with little warning. Rebuilt SDV6 engines from our specialists include uprated bearings, revised oil pumps, and full turbo assembly testing.

entries.title: 276DT 2.7 TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The 276DT was fitted to the Range Rover Sport (L320, 2005–2009) and Discovery 3. While less failure‑prone than the 3.0‑litre version, crankshaft bearing wear and oil pump housing failure still drive replacement, typically from 100,000 miles. Rebuilt units include upgraded bearings and a revised oil pump assembly.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 276DT Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 204DTD 2.0 Ingenium Diesel
entries.description: (150–180 HP) The 204DTD is used in the Range Rover Evoque (2015–2020), Velar (2017–present), and Discovery Sport. Timing chain stretch caused by oil dilution from incomplete DPF regenerations is the primary failure, often between 50,000 and 90,000 miles. Rebuilt units include upgraded timing chain kits, revised oil pumps, and thoroughly cleaned DPF systems.

entries.title: 204DTA 2.0 Ingenium Diesel
entries.description: (240 HP SD4/D240) The higher‑output 204DTA appears in the Evoque (2017–2020), Velar (2017–present), and Range Rover Sport (2016–2020). The same timing chain stretch and oil dilution issues affect this variant. A specialist rebuilt engine includes upgraded timing components, revised oil pumps, and full DPF system diagnostics.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTA Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 508PN 5.0 V8 Supercharged Petrol
entries.description: The 508PN powers the full-size Range Rover (L322, L405) and Range Rover Sport (L494, L320). Timing chain tensioner wear due to 10,000‑mile oil change intervals is the dominant failure, typically between 70,000 and 110,000 miles. Rebuilt units include reinforced tensioners, revised oil pumps, and a new supercharger coupler.

entries.title: AJ133 5.0 V8 Supercharged (High Output / SVR)
entries.description: The AJ133 high‑output variant is exclusive to the Range Rover Sport SVR and Autobiography models. In addition to timing chain tensioner failure, supercharger coupler degradation is common. Rebuilt AJ133 engines include reinforced timing components, upgraded supercharger couplers, and bench testing to factory power specifications.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 Range Rover SVR engine replacement

entries.title: 448PN 4.4 V8 Naturally Aspirated Petrol
entries.description: The 448PN (AJ41) was fitted to the L322 (2002–2009). This is a generally reliable unit, but VANOS issues and timing chain guide wear occur from 100,000+ miles. Rebuilt units include revised timing chain guides and VANOS service components.

entries.title: 448DT 4.4 SDV8 Diesel
entries.description: The 448DT is found in the Range Rover (L405, 2012–2017) and Range Rover Sport (L494, 2014–2017). Crankshaft fracture, coolant crossover pipe leaks, and turbo actuator faults drive replacement, typically from 90,000 miles. Rebuilt units include uprated crankshaft bearings, aluminium coolant pipes, and revised turbo actuators.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 448DT Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 368DT 3.6 TDV8 Diesel
entries.description: The 368DT powered the L322 (2006–2010). Crankshaft bearing fatigue and turbo actuator failure are the primary replacement drivers, typically between 90,000 and 140,000 miles. Rebuilt units include upgraded bearings, revised turbo actuators, and new EGR components.

entries.title: 306DTA 3.0 Ingenium Diesel
entries.description: (D250/D300) The 306DTA is used in the Range Rover (L405, late 2018–2022) and Range Rover Sport (L494, 2018–2022). Bearing wear and timing chain stretch are emerging failure points as these units age. Rebuilt engines from our network include upgraded timing chain kits and revised bearing shells.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DTA Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: P400e (PT204 + Electric
entries.description: Motor) 2.0 Plug‑in Hybrid The P400e hybrid combines the PT204 2.0 Ingenium petrol engine with an 85kW electric motor. Fitted to the Range Rover (L405, 2018–2022; L460, 2022–present) and Range Rover Sport (L494, 2018–2022). Timing chain failure on the petrol component and hybrid system controller faults drive specialist replacement demand. Rebuilt hybrid engines include upgraded timing components and full hybrid system diagnostics.
entries.cta: Compare P400e Range Rover hybrid engine prices

entries.title: 306D1 (M51) 2.5 TD Diesel
entries.description: The BMW M51 2.5‑litre turbo diesel powered the P38 Range Rover (1996–2002). Injection pump failure and turbo issues are the primary replacement drivers, typically after 100,000–150,000 miles. Rebuilt units include refurbished injection pumps and revised turbo oil feed lines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306D1 Range Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 4.6 Rover V8 Petrol
entries.description: The 4.6‑litre Rover V8 (46D) was fitted to the P38 (1994–2002). Cylinder liner slippage and head gasket failures are the most common replacement drivers, typically after 80,000–120,000 miles. Rebuilt units include liner fixes (top‑hat liners) and upgraded gaskets.
entries.cta: Compare 4.6 Rover V8 Range Rover engine prices

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Range Rover engine code directory below — every code from the last 30 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

engineYears.h2: Range Rover Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Introduction of GEMS (General Engine Management System) on petrol V8 variants, improving cold-start reliability

years.preview: P38a Era: BMW Diesel & Rover V8 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation Range Rover (P38a) marked the brand's first use of a BMW-sourced diesel unit alongside a heavily revised Rover V8, establishing a template of mixed-supplier powertrain strategy that would persist for two decades.
keyChanges: BMW 2.5-litre six-cylinder diesel (256T) replaced the ageing VM diesel, bringing genuine refinement to Range Rover diesel buyers
Rover 4.0 and 4.6-litre all-aluminium V8s replaced the carburettor-fed 3.9, gaining fuel injection across the range
Introduction of GEMS (General Engine Management System) on petrol V8 variants, improving cold-start reliability
Land Rover's first electronic engine management integrated with transmission control
mainEngines: 256T (BMW M51) - 2.5-litre six-cylinder diesel (Range Rover P38a)
14CUX/GEMS Rover V8 - 4.0 and 4.6-litre petrol (Range Rover P38a)
knownFor: BMW 2.5 diesel offers excellent refinement but head gasket failures are well-documented above 100,000 miles
Rover V8 aluminium block is lightweight and free-revving but prone to coolant loss and liner seal failure at high mileage
GEMS-managed V8s respond well to specialist rebuilds; parts supply remains strong via enthusiast network
P38a diesel buyers frequently report injector return-pipe degradation causing rough running and hard starting
years.cta: Search for 1994–2001 Range Rover P38a replacement engines on EngineMarket

years.preview: L322 Generation III Launch: Ford TD6 & Jaguar V8 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The L322 third-generation Range Rover arrived under Ford ownership and introduced two entirely new engine families - a BMW-derived 3.0 TD6 diesel and a Jaguar-sourced AJ-V8 petrol - transforming the model into a genuine luxury flagship.
keyChanges: Ford/BMW 3.0-litre TD6 diesel (M57) replaced the P38a's 2.5 six-cylinder, doubling torque availability
Jaguar AJ-V8 4.4-litre naturally aspirated petrol replaces Rover V8, bringing a more refined luxury-class unit
4.2-litre supercharged AJ-V8 introduced for Range Rover Sport (L320) at launch in 2005 - Land Rover's first factory-supercharged engine
ZF 5HP24 five-speed automatic transmission fitted across the range replacing the older four-speed unit
mainEngines: M57 (306D1 / TD6) - 3.0-litre six-cylinder diesel (Range Rover L322)
AJ-V8 4.4 - 4.4-litre naturally aspirated petrol (Range Rover L322)
AJ-V8 4.2SC - 4.2-litre supercharged petrol (Range Rover L322 Vogue SE, Range Rover Sport L320 from 2005)
popularModels: Range Rover L322 4.4 V8 Vogue SE
knownFor: TD6 M57 diesel is robust and long-lived but EGR valve fouling and swirl flap failures are common from 80,000 miles
AJ-V8 4.4 offers smooth delivery but known for timing chain stretch at high mileage if servicing intervals are extended
4.2 supercharged variant generates strong enthusiast demand; supercharger nose cone seal failures are a known weak point
Ford-era L322 benefits from broad specialist network and good parts availability in the UK
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Range Rover L322 3.0 TD6 engine replacement - Range Rover 4.4 V8 engine replacement - Range Rover L322 EGR failure engine - L322 AJ-V8 timing chain replacement
years.cta: Find replacement engines for your 2002–2005 Range Rover L322 - compare UK specialist quotes now

years.preview: Range Rover Sport L320 & L322 Facelift: TDV6 & TDV8 Entry Performance Landmark
years.description: The launch of the Range Rover Sport L320 in 2005 introduced the 2.7-litre TDV6 diesel to the Range Rover family and brought the 3.6-litre TDV8 in 2006 - expanding the diesel lineup into genuine performance diesel territory for the first time.
keyChanges: 2.7-litre Ford-sourced TDV6 (276DT) introduced in Range Rover Sport L320 at launch - the first V6 diesel in the family
3.6-litre TDV8 (368DT) introduced in Range Rover Sport 2006 and Range Rover L322 from 2007, producing up to 272 bhp
AJ133 5.0-litre naturally aspirated V8 introduced in L322 2005 facelift alongside the supercharged variant
L322 gains ZF 6HP26 six-speed automatic from 2006, improving both refinement and fuel economy
mainEngines: 276DT (TDV6) - 2.7-litre V6 diesel (Range Rover Sport L320)
368DT (TDV8) - 3.6-litre V8 diesel (Range Rover Sport L320, Range Rover L322)
AJ133 5.0 NA - 5.0-litre naturally aspirated V8 petrol (Range Rover L322 from 2005 facelift)
AJ-V8 4.2SC - 4.2-litre supercharged V8 (Range Rover Sport L320 HSE/Autobiography)
knownFor: TDV6 276DT is a proven Ford Duratorq unit - durable under normal service but injector seal leaks and EGR fouling emerge from 70,000 miles
TDV8 368DT offers exceptional diesel performance but chain tensioner and guide failures are documented on pre-2010 examples
Sport L320 4.2 Supercharged is a landmark performance SUV; supercharger oil seal failure causes blue smoke and premature unit wear
Timing chain issues on early TDV8 units prompted investigation and revised tensioners across the fleet
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Range Rover Sport TDV6 engine replacement - Range Rover 3.6 TDV8 timing chain failure - Range Rover Sport L320 engine replacement - Range Rover TDV8 engine for sale UK
years.cta: Compare replacement TDV6 and TDV8 engines for your 2005–2009 Range Rover Sport or L322

years.preview: Facelift Era: TDV8 Evolution, SDV6 & AJ133 SC Performance Landmark
years.description: The 2009–2010 facelifts across L322 and L320 Sport ushered in the 5.0-litre AJ133 supercharged V8 - one of the most desirable and widely sought-after Range Rover performance engines - alongside an upgraded 3.6 TDV8 and the transitional 3.0 SDV6.
keyChanges: AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 (508PN/508PS) introduced as new performance flagship across L322 and L320, producing 510 bhp in final tune
3.0-litre SDV6 (306DT) replaces 2.7 TDV6 in Range Rover Sport from 2011 MY, bringing Euro 5 compliance and improved torque
3.6 TDV8 revised with updated chain tensioners addressing documented failures from the pre-facelift units
ZF 6HP automatic replaced by ZF 8HP eight-speed across the range, significantly improving fuel economy and shift quality
mainEngines: AJ133 5.0SC (508PN / 508PS) - 5.0-litre supercharged V8 petrol (Range Rover L322, Range Rover Sport L320)
306DT (SDV6) - 3.0-litre V6 diesel (Range Rover Sport L320 from 2011)
368DT (TDV8) - 3.6-litre V8 diesel (Range Rover Sport L320, continuing)
276DT (TDV6) - 2.7-litre diesel (Range Rover Sport run-out)
knownFor: AJ133 5.0SC is a precision-engineered unit delivering supercar-grade performance in a luxury SUV; supercharger drive belt and intercooler water pump are known service items
SDV6 306DT brings meaningful economy improvements over the TDV8 but crankshaft bearing failure has been documented on high-mileage units - a significant cost risk
TDV8 revised tensioner fix resolved the most critical chain issues; well-maintained examples now regarded as dependable long-haulers
ZF 8HP transmission pairing is widely regarded as one of the finest automatic gearboxes ever fitted to a Land Rover product
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Range Rover 5.0 Supercharged engine replacement - Range Rover Sport 3.0 SDV6 crankshaft failure - Range Rover L322 5.0SC engine for sale - SDV6 engine replacement cost UK
years.cta: Get specialist quotes for 2009–2012 Range Rover 5.0 SC and SDV6 replacement engines

years.preview: L405 & L494 Sport Launch: SDV8, TDV6 & Hybrid Introduction Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The all-aluminium fourth-generation Range Rover L405 and companion L494 Sport arrived in 2012–2013 as the most technologically ambitious Land Rover products to date, introducing the 4.4-litre SDV8, a recalibrated SDV6, and the brand's first-ever diesel hybrid drivetrain.
keyChanges: 4.4-litre SDV8 (448DT) introduced as flagship diesel in L405 and L494, producing 339 bhp with parallel-sequential turbocharging
SDV6 (306DT) carried over but recalibrated for lighter all-aluminium body - now the most popular spec in the range
Range Rover L405 Hybrid launched 2014 - Land Rover's first diesel hybrid, pairing 3.0 SDV6 with an electric motor
Euro 6 emissions compliance from 2015–2016 MY introduces revised fuel injection mapping and DPF updates
TDV6 (276DT) discontinued in this generation; SDV6 and SDV8 carry all diesel volumes
mainEngines: 448DT (SDV8) - 4.4-litre V8 diesel (Range Rover L405, Range Rover Sport L494)
306DT (SDV6) - 3.0-litre V6 diesel (Range Rover L405, Range Rover Sport L494, Range Rover Velar from 2017)
AJ133 5.0SC (508PN / 508PS) - 5.0-litre supercharged V8 petrol (Range Rover L405, Range Rover Sport L494)
306PS (3.0 V6 petrol supercharged) - (Range Rover Sport L494 from 2013)
popularModels: Range Rover Sport L494 3.0 V6 SC
knownFor: SDV6 crankshaft bearing failure is the dominant failure mode in L405 and L494 - documented from as low as 60,000 miles; specialist advice is to check oil pressure proactively
SDV8 448DT is a powerful and refined diesel but shares some bearing wear patterns with the SDV6 at high mileage
AJ133 5.0SC carries over proven architecture; supercharger and water pump are the primary service targets on ageing units
L405 Hybrid diesel adds complexity around battery pack conditioning and hybrid system maintenance that independent specialists are now well-equipped to handle
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Range Rover L405 SDV6 engine replacement - Range Rover Sport SDV6 crankshaft failure - Range Rover 4.4 SDV8 engine replacement - Land Rover 3.0 TDV6 crankshaft repair UK - Range Rover L405 engine for sale
years.cta: Compare prices for 2012–2017 Range Rover L405 and Sport L494 replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Ingenium 2.0 Petrol & Diesel Arrives in Sport & Velar Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The 2017 model year brought Land Rover's in-house Ingenium four-cylinder family into the Sport and the new Velar, ending the brand's reliance on external-supplier engines in the mid-range and bringing Euro 6 compliance as standard.
keyChanges: Ingenium AJ20D4 2.0-litre diesel introduced in Range Rover Sport L494 and Range Rover Velar L560 - first Ingenium unit in full-size Range Rover family
Ingenium AJ20P4 2.0-litre petrol launched simultaneously in Velar and Sport, replacing the 2.0 Si4 turbocharged Ford-derived unit
Range Rover Evoque L538 transitions fully to Ingenium family by 2015–2016, preceding Sport and Velar
Discovery Sport (L550) runs full Ingenium lineup from 2015 - confirming Ingenium as the cross-brand standard for four-cylinder
P400e plug-in hybrid (PHEV) platform confirmed for next phase, previewed in this period
mainEngines: AJ20D4 (Ingenium 2.0 diesel) - 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel (Range Rover Sport L494, Range Rover Velar L560, Range Rover Evoque L538, Discovery Sport L550)
AJ20P4 (Ingenium 2.0 petrol) - 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol (Range Rover Velar, Range Rover Sport, Evoque, Discovery Sport)
306DT (SDV6) - 3.0-litre diesel continues in L405 and L494
508PN / 508PS - 5.0SC continues in L405 and L494
popularModels: Range Rover Sport L494 2.0 Ingenium D240
Range Rover Velar L560 2.0 D180 / D240
Range Rover Evoque L538 2.0 eD4 / TD4
Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 2.0 Ingenium
knownFor: Ingenium AJ20D4 delivers class-leading fuel economy but early units (pre-2018) suffer from EGR cooler cracking and coolant contamination - a serious and well-documented failure
Timing chain tensioner wear on early AJ20D4 units is a known recurring theme; specialist rebuilders now offer uprated tensioner kits
AJ20P4 petrol is regarded as more robust than the diesel equivalent; carbon build-up on intake ports is an evolving long-term complaint
Ingenium family marks Land Rover's first genuinely in-house modern engine family - a heritage moment for the brand with real long-term parts security
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Range Rover 2.0 Ingenium diesel engine replacement - Range Rover Velar 2.0D engine failure - Discovery Sport Ingenium engine replacement UK - Range Rover Evoque 2.0 TD4 engine for sale - AJ20D4 timing chain replacement
years.cta: Get 2017–2019 Range Rover Ingenium engine replacement quotes - compare UK rebuilt and remanufactured options

years.preview: PHEV, Ingenium Inline-Six & P400e Technology Introduction
years.description: This period delivered Land Rover's first-generation plug-in hybrid across multiple Range Rover models alongside the landmark straight-six Ingenium engine - signalling a decisive shift in the brand's powertrain philosophy toward electrification.
keyChanges: P400e PHEV (PT204 / AJ20P4 + electric motor) launched in Range Rover L405 and Range Rover Sport L494 from 2018 - Land Rover's first production PHEV
Ingenium AJ30 inline-six (3.0-litre six-cylinder MHEV) introduced in Range Rover Sport HST 2019, producing 400 PS
48V mild-hybrid (MHEV) technology introduced across AJ20D4 diesel lineup from 2019–2020 MY
Second-generation Range Rover Evoque (L551) launched 2019 with full Ingenium lineup including MHEV option
SDV8 448DT phased out from L405 by 2018; SDV6 becomes sole diesel for the full-size Range Rover

AJ30 Ingenium I6 (P400 MHEV) - 3.0-litre straight-six mild hybrid (Range Rover Sport L494 HST/Autobiography from 2019)
AJ20D4 MHEV - 2.0-litre diesel mild hybrid (Range Rover Evoque L551, Discovery Sport L550 from 2019)
AJ20P4 MHEV - 2.0-litre petrol mild hybrid (Evoque L551, Discovery Sport from 2020)

Range Rover Sport L494 P400 HST

knownFor: P400e PHEV battery pack degradation and charge port module failures are the emerging concerns - with specialist refurbishment now available
Ingenium I6 AJ30 is widely praised for its combination of refinement, torque, and efficiency; early units show strong reliability in specialist feedback
48V MHEV belt-integrated starter-generator (BISG) adds an additional maintenance item but is generally regarded as a reliable system in normal UK use
EGR cooler issues from the AJ20D4 carry forward into MHEV variants - owners of 2019–2020 diesel MHEV models should proactively inspect cooler condition

years.preview: Generation V (L460) & L461 Evoque: Full Ingenium, PHEV Standard & V8 Return Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The fifth-generation Range Rover L460 launched in 2022 with a modernised Ingenium lineup as standard fit, PHEV across all primary grades, and the reintroduction of a BMW-sourced V8 petrol - marking both a technological leap and a deliberate nod to the brand's luxury heritage.
keyChanges: All-new L460 body-on-aluminium platform introduced with Ingenium AJ30 3.0-litre inline-six as the core engine across all trim levels
P530 4.4-litre twin-turbocharged BMW V8 petrol (N63 / B44) introduced in Range Rover L460 SV - the first V8 since the AJ133 era
P460e and P510e plug-in hybrids offered as standard-fit options across the L460 range, not as limited derivatives
Range Rover Velar updated with full Ingenium lineup; Evoque L551 continues MHEV programme
Electric Range Rover (Electric) confirmed for 2025 release - first all-electric Range Rover
mainEngines: AJ30 I6 MHEV (P360 / P400) - 3.0-litre mild-hybrid inline-six petrol (Range Rover L460, Range Rover Sport L461)
D300 I6 MHEV - 3.0-litre mild-hybrid inline-six diesel (Range Rover L460, Range Rover Sport L461)
P460e / P510e PHEV - 3.0-litre inline-six + electric (Range Rover L460)
BMW N63/B44 4.4 V8 TT - 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 petrol (Range Rover L460 SV P530)
AJ20D4 / AJ20P4 MHEV - 2.0-litre four-cylinder (Range Rover Evoque L551, Discovery Sport L550, Velar continued)
popularModels: Range Rover L460 SV P530 V8
Range Rover Sport L461 P360 MHEV
Range Rover Sport L461 P400 MHEV
knownFor: AJ30 I6 MHEV is the refinement benchmark for the current Range Rover range; early L460 examples show strong reliability but air suspension and software calibration complaints are logged
BMW N63 V8 brings genuine grand-touring character to the SV - early owner sentiment is very positive; inherited BMW-side oil consumption tendencies are a known long-term watch point
PHEV models deliver strong electric-only range (circa 60 miles) in real-world UK use; high-voltage battery pack longevity remains the key question for future replacement demand
L461 Sport shares drivetrain completely with L460 - owners of both models benefit from identical engine parts compatibility and shared specialist knowledge

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Range Rover was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Range Rover specialists.

faq.h2: Range Rover Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Range Rover owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Range Rover engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement Range Rover engine typically costs between £1,800 for a used 2.2-litre Evoque unit and £9,500 for a fully reconditioned 5.0 V8 supercharged with upgraded timing components. Reconditioned 3.0 SDV6 diesels – the most commonly replaced engine – range from £3,500 to £6,500 supply & fit. Used engines start around £800 for older units but carry higher repeat failure risk. Fitting adds £500–£1,500 depending on model and specialist location. Compare three quotes minimum – prices on the same engine code can vary by over £1,000.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Range Rover engine replacement?
items.answer: Four factors drive price: engine size and rarity (5.0 V8 costs more than 2.0 diesel), condition (reconditioned with upgrades costs more than used), vehicle complexity (L405 takes longer than L322), and supply-only versus supply & fit. Location also matters – London specialists typically charge 20–30% more than Midlands or Northern England workshops. EngineMarket's quote tool captures all these variables so you compare like-for-like. Always ask whether the quoted price includes fitting, delivery, and VAT.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Range Rover engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Range Rover?
items.answer: In most cases, yes – especially if the chassis, gearbox, and body are solid. A reconditioned SDV6 with uprated bearings costs £4,000–£6,500 fitted, compared to spending £15,000–£30,000 on a replacement vehicle of similar age and specification. Rebuilt engines with upgraded components often outlast the original factory units because they address the known design flaw. For high-value models like L405 Autobiography or L494 SVR, replacement is almost always financially sensible. For very high-mileage L320 Sports worth under £6,000, a good used engine may be the smarter short-term option.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Range Rover engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Range Rover engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply & fit package includes: sourcing a reconditioned or used engine matched to your VIN, removing the failed unit, fitting the replacement with new gaskets, seals, fluids, and filters, proper disposal of the old engine, and a road test to verify operation. Most specialists include a 12-month workmanship warranty on fitting alongside the engine's parts warranty. Supply-only costs £500–£1,500 less but leaves you to arrange fitting locally. Always confirm whether ancillaries like turbochargers, injectors, and the oil pump are transferred or replaced.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Range Rover engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common Range Rover models like the L322, L405, L494, or L320 Sport with readily available engine codes (3.0 SDV6, 4.4 TDV8, 5.0 V8), a like-for-like swap takes 3–5 working days. More complex models, hybrid variants, or rare engine codes can take 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability – our network of 150+ UK specialists stocks the most commonly replaced units, but niche or older engines may add 2–3 days for delivery. Ask your supplier for a confirmed timeline before committing.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover engine lead times and prices side by side

items.question: Do reconditioned Range Rover engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes – every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Reconditioned and remanufactured units from premium specialists often include extended 24-month coverage. The warranty typically covers major internal components: crankshaft, bearings, pistons, timing chain, oil pump, and cylinder head. Wear items like belts, gaskets, and filters are usually covered under the fitting warranty (labour) not the parts warranty. Extended warranties beyond 24 months are sometimes available on rebuilt units with upgraded components. Always confirm exact terms – including labour coverage – with your chosen supplier before purchase.
items.cta: Get warranty-secured Range Rover engine quotes now

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Range Rover engine?
items.answer: Used engines cost £800–£4,000 and work well as a short-term fix – ideal if you plan to sell the vehicle within 12 months or have a very tight budget. However, used SDV6 or TDV6 units carry the same crankshaft risk as your failed engine. Reconditioned engines cost £2,800–£9,500 but include upgraded components (uprated bearings, reinforced chains, revised oil pumps), full bench testing, and a 12–24 month warranty. If you plan to keep your Range Rover for 3+ years or cover high annual mileage, reconditioned with upgrades is almost always better value long-term.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Range Rover engine makes financial sense

items.question: Where can I find Range Rover engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK-wide network of 150+ specialists who focus specifically on Range Rover, Land Rover, and premium SUV engine replacement. While the Midlands (Birmingham, Coventry, Leicester) has a concentration of Land Rover specialists due to heritage, our network covers every region including London, Manchester, Leeds, Bristol, Glasgow, and Belfast. Enter your registration or postcode, and we'll match you with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code and offer supply & fit. Nationwide delivery is also available if you have a trusted local garage for fitting.
items.cta: Find Range Rover engine specialists near you with live quotes

items.question: What are the most common Range Rover engine failures?
items.answer: The most common catastrophic failures driving replacement: 3.0 SDV6/TDV6 crankshaft bearing fatigue (snapped crank, L405, L494, L320), 5.0 V8 timing chain tensioner wear (L322, L405, Sport), 4.4 TDV8 crankshaft fracture and turbo actuator failure, 2.0 Ingenium timing chain stretch (Evoque, Velar), and 2.7 TDV6 plastic oil pump housing fracture (Sport L320). Early detection rarely prevents these failures – they typically cause sudden, irreversible engine death. Replacement with a reconditioned unit containing upgraded components is the only permanent solution.
items.cta: Compare replacement prices for common Range Rover problem engines

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Range Rover engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes – prices vary by £1,000+ on identical engine codes across different specialists. Used engines offer the lowest upfront cost but check service history and mileage. Reconditioned with upgraded components costs more but delivers better long-term value on vehicles you plan to keep. Supply-only saves £500–£1,500 if you have a trusted local garage. Always ask: is delivery included? Is VAT included? Is the 12-month warranty parts-only or parts-and-labour? Does the price include new gaskets and fluids? EngineMarket does this comparison for you – free, no obligation.
items.cta: Compare Range Rover engine prices across UK specialists now
